topics.topic_title: Introduction to Travel and Tourism Industry

sub_topics.title: Travel and Tourism in Zambia
competences: Interpret the history of Tourism in Zambia
Analyse career development in the Tourism industry

instructional_blocks.hook: Begin by asking some guiding questions: Have you ever travelled to another town or country? What comes to your mind when you hear the words 'travel' and 'tourism'? Who do you think a tourist is?
teacher_steps: Display pictures or videos showing different types of tourists and tourism activities in Zambia
Lead discussion on domestic and international tourists in Zambia
learner_steps: Pair Work: Classify if person is a tourist, excursionist, host, or guest
Role Play: Act as tourists and hosts (hotel receptionist, safari guide, local vendor)
Discuss and share examples of domestic and international tourist in Zambia

instructional_blocks.hook: What comes to mind when you think of tourism? Have you ever visited any tourist attraction in Zambia? Where?
teacher_steps: Show images of Zambia's top tourist attractions (Victoria Falls, South Luangwa, Lower Zambezi)
Explain tourism evolution from early explorers to modern tourism industry
Divide class into groups for research and presentation
Facilitate role play and quiz game
learner_steps: Group Work: Research and present on early history, national parks, attractions, packages, or modern tourism
Role Play: Act as tourists or tour guides explaining Zambia's attractions
Participate in quiz on Zambia's tourism history and attractions

instructional_blocks.hook: Research a specific historical site in Zambia
teacher_steps: Suggest key historical sites (Victoria Falls, Livingstone Museum, Shiwa Ng'andu Estate, Mwela Rock Paintings, etc.)
Guide research process using books, articles, internet
Facilitate interviews with teachers, parents, or community elders
learner_steps: Gather information on location, historical background, significance, key events/figures, current status
Create presentation (written report, poster, oral presentation, or creative project)

sub_topics.short_notes:
Tourism in Zambia started with wildlife conservation. The establishment of national parks and game reserves (South Luangwa, Kafue, Lower Zambezi) marked early development. Post-independence saw growth of local tourism. Modern tourism includes development of lodges, hotels, adventure tourism, and eco-tourism. Traditional ceremonies (Kuomboka, Nc'wala) play important roles in attracting tourists.

Historical Evolution: Tourism in early times included hunters and explorers like David Livingstone. The Colonial era saw game reserves and national parks established. Post-independence brought growth of local tourism. Modern tourism features lodges, hotels, adventure tourism, and eco-tourism.

Zambia's Tourist Attractions include: Natural attractions (national parks, waterfalls, lakes, rivers like Victoria Falls, Chishimba falls), Cultural attractions (traditional ceremonies, historical sites like Mukuni Village, Choma Museum), and Man-made attractions (hotels, lodges, safari camps).

Tourist Packages: Adventure tourism (bungee jumping, safaris, white-water rafting), Cultural tourism (visiting traditional ceremonies, museums), Eco-tourism (national parks, birdwatching, nature walks), and Luxury tourism (five-star lodges, cruises on the Zambezi River).

Modern Tourism Trends in Zambia: Increased use of social media and online bookings, Sustainable tourism (wildlife conservation, eco-lodges), Domestic tourism promotion by government, and Event-based tourism (festivals, sports events, Mosi Day of Thunder).

vocabulary.definition: Activities of people traveling to and staying in places outside their usual environment for leisure, business, or other purposes
Businesses and organizations involved in providing goods and services to tourists
The past of tourism in Zambia, from its early beginnings to its current state
Changes and transformations that have occurred within the tourism industry in Zambia over time
Places and activities that draw tourists to a destination
Pre-arranged travel arrangements combining various travel components at a discounted price
Contemporary trends, technologies, and practices within the tourism industry
Job opportunities and professional development paths available within the tourism industry
A detailed plan or schedule of activities, events, travel arrangement
Moving from one place to another
A person who travels away from home for at least 24 hours for leisure, business, or other reasons
A person who visits a place for less than 24 hours
A person travelling within their own country
A person from another country visiting a destination
People who provide services to tourists
Traveller who engages into sleeping accommodation and uses other services of the host

questions.description: Tour Guide Role-Play: Learners act as tour guides, explaining a selected Zambian tourist attraction to a group. Deliver a 5-10 minutes guided tour with storytelling and factual details.

Write a 2-3 page research report on a selected historical site in Zambia: Describe the site's historical background, explain its role in tourism development, discuss its importance to Zambia's economy and cultural heritage, identify challenges and suggest improvements for tourism.
Visit a local tourist attraction or historical site. Write a report on its tourism significance and visitor experience.

sub_topics.title: Explore forms of tourism

instructional_blocks.hook: Have you or someone you know ever traveled to another place for fun, work, or learning? What was the purpose of the trip? Do you think all tourists travel for the same reasons?
teacher_steps: Take note of learners' responses and categorize them under different types of tourism
Divide learners into small groups and assign topics
Summarize and clarify key points with real-life examples from Zambia and beyond

instructional_blocks.hook: Why do people travel for tourism? How does tourism benefit a country?
teacher_steps: Let learners discuss in pairs and share their ideas
Take note key points (jobs, income, culture, environment)
Divide class into groups for research
Summarize and clarify key points after each presentation
learner_steps: Discuss in pairs about tourism benefits
Research assigned topic (Economic, Cultural Exchange, or Environmental Impact)

sub_topics.short_notes:
Tourism refers to the movement of people from one place to another for leisure, business, or other purposes, usually for a short period.

Types of Tourism:
- Domestic Tourism: When people travel within their own country (e.g., family from Lusaka visiting Victoria Falls)
- International Tourism: When people travel to a different country (e.g., tourist from South Africa visiting Zambia)
- Inbound Tourism: When tourists from other countries visit a particular country (e.g., German tourist visiting Zambia)
- Outbound Tourism: When people from a country travel to another country (e.g., Zambian traveling to Dubai)

Forms of Tourism:
- Leisure/Holiday Tourism: Travel for relaxation and enjoyment (beach, safari trips, adventure tourism)
- Business Tourism: Travel for work-related purposes (trade, meetings, incentive travel, conventions)
- Cultural Tourism: Travel to experience different cultures, traditions, and historical sites (museums, landmarks, ceremonies)
- Ecotourism: Travel focusing on natural environments and conservation efforts (game parks, nature reserves, birdwatching)

Importance of Tourism:
Economic: Creates employment, generates income through foreign exchange, supports local businesses, encourages infrastructure development
Social and Cultural: Promotes cultural exchange, helps preserve cultural heritage, enhances national pride
Environmental: Supports conservation efforts, encourages sustainable tourism practices, raises awareness about environmental protection
Political and Educational: Strengthens international relations, provides learning opportunities about history, geography, and different cultures

vocabulary.definition: Travel for relaxation and enjoyment
Travel for work-related purposes such as trade, meetings, or conferences
Travel to experience different cultures, traditions, and historical sites
Travel that focuses on natural environments and conservation efforts

sub_topics.title: Analyse Career Development in the Tourism Industry
competences: Analyse Career Development in the Tourism industry

instructional_blocks.hook: What do you think are some jobs in the tourism industry?
teacher_steps: Briefly introduce the tourism industry as a major employer
Divide class into small groups and assign sectors
learner_steps: Group Work: Research/discuss jobs in assigned sector
Identify careers, roles, responsibilities, and required skills

instructional_blocks.hook: Visit tourism industry locations for real-world observation
teacher_steps: Arrange field visits to hotels, airports, tourist attractions, or restaurants
Organize Q&A sessions with industry workers
learner_steps: Ask workers about jobs, required skills, and challenges
Write report or create presentation on field experience

sub_topics.short_notes:
Job/Business Opportunities in Tourism:

Tour and Travel Services: Tour Guide, Travel Consultant, Travel Agent, Meeting/Event Planner, Tour Operator, Heritage Site Manager, Tourism Entrepreneur, Eco-Tourism Guide

Travel and Transportation: Airline Pilot, Flight Attendant, Cruise Ship Crew, Cabin Crew Supervisor, Ticketing and Reservation Officer, Chauffeur/Limousine Driver, Car Rental Agent, Railway Services Manager, Logistics Manager

Lodging (Hotels, Resorts, Lodges): Receptionist, Concierge, Housekeeper, Hotel Manager, Front Desk Supervisor, Bellhop/Porter, Resort Animator/Entertainment Coordinator, Room Service Attendant, Laundry Services Manager

Food and Beverage Services: Waiter/Waitress, Chef, Bartender, Restaurant Manager, Catering Manager, Pastry Chef/Baker, Sommelier (Wine Steward), Barista, Food Vendor, Food Safety Inspector

vocabulary.definition: Leads tourists and provides information about attractions
Books flights, accommodations, and packages for travelers
Designs and sells tour packages
Assists guests with local activities, reservations, and recommendations
Wine steward who advises on and serves wine

questions.description: Research and create a timeline of the major events in the history of tourism in Zambia, from pre-colonial times to the present day
Imagine you are a tourism developer in Zambia in the 1960s. Create a plan for developing tourism in the country, including infrastructure and attractions
Showcase and demonstrate a tourist guide, providing information on historical sites and attractions in Zambia
Write an essay on evolution of Tourism in Zambia
Case study on the development of Victoria falls as a tourist attraction

sub_topics.title: Demonstrate understanding of travel and tourism elements
competences: Demonstrate understanding of travel and tourism elements

instructional_blocks.hook: What do you think are the most important things needed for tourism to happen?
teacher_steps: Assign each group one element to discuss
learner_steps: Provide real-life examples from Zambia and beyond

instructional_blocks.hook: What do you think makes a place attractive for tourists?
teacher_steps: Guide learners towards identifying aspects related to accessibility, attractions, accommodation, amenities, and activities
learner_steps: Brainstorm about what makes places attractive
Research or brainstorm examples of assigned 'A' using local sites

instructional_blocks.hook: Identify and label tourism components on a map
teacher_steps: Guide learners to identify examples of each 'A'
learner_steps: Label at least one example of each 'A' on the map
Identify Attraction, Accessibility, Accommodation, Amenities, and Activities

sub_topics.short_notes:
Travel and tourism consist of key elements that work together to create a complete tourism experience:

Transportation: Enables movement of tourists via air, road, rail, and water. Includes Air Transport (Airlines, chartered flights), Land Transport (Buses, trains, taxis, rental cars, bikes, bicycles), Water Transport (Cruises, ferries, boats), Specialty Transport (Cable cars, bicycles, animal-drawn transport). Importance: Provides accessibility, determines ease and comfort, influences cost of travel.

Accommodation: Place where tourists stay during their trip. Types include Hotels (budget to luxury), Motels (roadside lodgings), Resorts (leisure-focused with entertainment), Lodges and Guesthouses (smaller, family-run), Hostels and Camping Sites (budget-friendly). Role: Provides comfort and security, contributes to tourism experience, generates employment and revenue.

Attractions: Places or events that draw visitors. Types include Natural Attractions (national parks, waterfalls, beaches), Cultural Attractions (museums, historical sites, festivals), Built Attractions (theme parks, skyscrapers, monuments), Events and Festivals (music concerts, sporting events, religious gatherings). Importance: Boosts tourism, preserves culture and history, generates income.

Tour Operators and Travel Agencies: Intermediaries assisting in planning and booking. Roles include designing travel itineraries, offering guided tours, providing transportation and accommodation arrangements, booking flights and hotels, providing travel advice and visa assistance, selling travel insurance.

Tourism Services and Hospitality: Support services enhancing traveler's experience. Examples include Hospitality Services (restaurants, catering, customer care), Banking and Currency Exchange, Safety and Security Services, Entertainment and Leisure. Importance: Ensures customer satisfaction, provides necessary support, enhances tourism experience.

Competitive Components of Tourism (The Five 'A's):
1. Attractions: Main reasons tourists visit (natural, cultural, man-made)
2. Accessibility: How easy it is to reach destination (transport modes, travel documents)
3. Accommodation: Lodging options (hotels, lodges, budget accommodations)
4. Amenities: Essential services and facilities (food & drink, healthcare, banking, security, shopping, communication)
5. Activities: Things tourists can do (wildlife safaris, water activities, cultural experiences, adventure sports)

vocabulary.definition: Locations that tourists visit for leisure, business, or cultural experiences
Places or activities that draw tourists to visit a destination
The friendly and welcoming treatment of guests or tourists
Lodging or places where tourists stay during their trip
The means of moving people from one place to another
Free time when people relax and enjoy activities of their choice
Activities that people do for enjoyment and relaxation
Companies or individuals who organize and sell travel packages
Essential services and facilities available to tourists
How easy it is for tourists to reach a destination

questions.description: Design and create a poster/brochure on a destination of choice with information about attraction, accommodation, amenities, accessibility and activities
Essay questions interpreting the history of Travel and Tourism
Written exercise on core elements of Travel and Tourism
Quiz on the forms of Tourism
Short answers on 5A's of Tourism

sub_topics.title: Describing skills needed in the tourism industry
competences: Describe skills needed in the tourism industry

instructional_blocks.hook: Imagine you are working in a hotel or tour company. What skills do you think you would need to be successful?
teacher_steps: Have learners discuss in pairs and share answers
Introduce Interpersonal Skills and Intrapersonal Skills
Use real-life tourism scenarios to explain each skill
learner_steps: Participate in role-playing as tourist and tour guide
Discuss solutions to problem-solving scenario in small groups

sub_topics.short_notes:
Skills needed in tourism industry fall into two categories:

Interpersonal Skills (Working with others effectively):
- Communication: Clear speaking, listening, and writing (e.g., helping tourists with directions)
- Teamwork: Collaborating with colleagues in hotels, airlines, or tour companies
- Cultural Sensitivity: Understanding different customs and traditions to serve tourists better
- Customer Service: Being friendly and helpful to ensure customer satisfaction

Intrapersonal Skills (Managing oneself effectively):
- Self-Awareness: Understanding personal strengths and weaknesses in tourism roles
- Adaptability: Being flexible when plans change (e.g., flight delays or weather disruptions)
- Self-Management: Being responsible and professional at work

vocabulary.definition: Skills for working with others effectively
Skills for managing oneself effectively
Clear speaking, listening, and writing
Collaborating with colleagues
Understanding different customs and traditions
Understanding personal strengths and weaknesses
Being flexible when plans change

questions.description: Write down one interpersonal and one intrapersonal skill you need to improve and why

sub_topics.title: Practicing planning a tourism itinerary
competences: Practice planning a tourism itinerary based on specific types of tourism

instructional_blocks.hook: If you had visitors coming to your town, what interesting places would you take them to?
teacher_steps: Assign each group a type of tourism
learner_steps: Share ideas about local interesting places
Include transport, accommodation, meals, and activities
Cover Day 1 (arrival, welcome, accommodation), Day 2 (main activities), Day 3 (final activities, departure)

sub_topics.short_notes:
Importance of Planning for a Smooth Tourist Experience:

1. Ensures Proper Budgeting: Helps determine needed money, prevents overspending, allows for discounts

2. Saves Time: Effective scheduling, reduces wasted time looking for services

3. Ensures Availability of Services: Guarantees accommodation and attraction bookings, avoids disappointment

4. Enhances Safety and Security: Research safe destinations, prepare vaccinations and insurance, have emergency contacts

5. Allows for Better Experience: Plan activities matching interests, select best restaurants and locations

6. Helps Understanding Local Culture and Laws: Research traditions and customs, prevents misunderstandings

7. Ensures Balanced Itinerary: Avoids overloading schedule, creates mix of relaxation and adventure

vocabulary.definition: A detailed plan or schedule of activities, events, and travel arrangements
Visiting historical and cultural sites
Nature-based travel
Exciting activities like bungee jumping and rafting
Traveling to religious sites for spiritual reasons
Travelling for medical treatment or wellness therapies

questions.description: Groups exchange itineraries and provide feedback on clarity, organization, and creativity
What was the most challenging part of planning the itinerary? How does this itinerary meet the needs of tourists? What improvements would you make next time?
Were all group members engaged? Did students effectively incorporate tourism concepts? Was the itinerary practical and realistic?

sub_topics.title: Tourists' codes of behaviour

instructional_blocks.hook: What comes to mind when you think of tourism? Have you ever visited any tourist attraction in Zambia? Where?
teacher_steps: Discuss behaviors expected in different cultural and tourist settings
Create a list of do's and don'ts for responsible tourism
Facilitate group discussion on respecting local cultures
Examine case studies of irresponsible tourist behavior
learner_steps: Brainstorm expected behaviors in different settings
Discuss importance of respecting local cultures
Research travel challenges for special needs tourists

instructional_blocks.hook: Visit a local cultural site to observe customs and traditions
teacher_steps: Organize field trip to cultural site, museum, or traditional village
Organize cultural dress and etiquette simulation
learner_steps: Visit cultural site and observe customs
Learn proper table manners and dining customs

instructional_blocks.hook: Identify and solve travel risk scenarios
teacher_steps: Provide scenarios of travelers facing risks
learner_steps: Identify risks and suggest preventive measures
Act out scenarios (tourist robbed, ill, emergency)

instructional_blocks.hook: Design travel solutions for special needs tourists
teacher_steps: Guide design of inclusive travel brochure
Arrange guest speaker or virtual interview
learner_steps: Discuss potential challenges in small groups
Include special services, recommendations, and healthcare facilities

instructional_blocks.hook: Explore ethical codes of behavior for tourists
teacher_steps: Assign research project on tourism destination
Facilitate group project on responsible travel guide
learner_steps: Create responsible travel guide for specific destination

sub_topics.short_notes:
Responsible tourism is about respecting places visited and people who live there. Tourist Code of Behavior provides guidelines for traveling responsibly and sustainably.

Respect for Local Culture: Preserves identity, traditions, and values. Key considerations include: Understanding local customs (dress codes, greetings, table manners), Cultural sensitivity (avoid offensive behaviors), Participating respectfully in cultural events, Learning basic local language phrases, Supporting local culture (engage with arts, crafts, traditions).

Significance of Respecting Local Cultures and Traditions:
- Promotes Mutual Understanding and Respect
- Enhances the Tourist Experience
- Preserves Cultural Heritage
- Supports Ethical and Sustainable Tourism
- Builds a Positive Image of Tourists

Ways to Respect Local Cultures: Learn about culture before traveling, Dress modestly and appropriately, Use local language basics, Respect religious and social practices, Be mindful of photography, Follow local dining etiquette, Support local businesses and artisans.

Consequences of Disrespecting: Offending local community, Legal consequences, Negative tourist reputation, Cultural erosion.

Risks and Concerns While Traveling:

1. Crime and Personal Security: Common crimes include pickpocketing, scams, identity theft, assault, kidnapping. How to reduce: Avoid displaying valuables, Use secure bags, Be cautious of strangers, Research common scams, Stay in well-lit areas, Keep copies of documents.

2. Financial Risks: Losing money/cards, Overspending, Unexpected costs. How to reduce: Carry multiple payment forms, Store emergency cash separately, Set daily budget, Use travel insurance, Avoid public Wi-Fi for banking.

3. Health Concerns: Food poisoning, Infectious diseases, Allergies, Fatigue/jet lag. How to reduce: Get vaccinations, Drink bottled water, Carry first aid kit, Wear appropriate clothing, Maintain hygiene, Have health insurance.

4. Travel Logistics: Missing transportation, Lost luggage, Getting lost, Unsafe transport. How to reduce: Plan and book in advance, Arrive early, Keep document copies, Use navigation apps, Choose reputable services.

5. Emergency Situations: Political instability, Natural disasters, Terrorist attacks. How to reduce: Stay informed, Register with embassy, Learn emergency contacts, Follow authorities' instructions.

Tourists with Special Needs:

Elderly Travelers: Need mobility assistance (wheelchairs, ramps, elevators), Health & safety (medical services, clear signage), Spacious accommodation with grab bars, Priority seating in transportation, Clear communication (large fonts), Relaxing activities.

Pregnant Women: Need comfortable seating, Medical support nearby, Healthy food options, Extra transportation assistance, Accessible accommodation.

Children and Infants: Need child-friendly facilities (cribs, high chairs, play areas), Safety measures (child-proofing), Transportation needs (strollers, booster seats), Age-appropriate entertainment, Children's menus, Child-friendly medical services. For infants: Baby essentials allowed, Changing tables, Quiet feeding areas.

General Tips: Ensure staff training in communication, Use accessible booking systems, Inclusive marketing materials.

Tourist's Codes of Behaviour:

1. Respect for Local Culture: Understanding customs, Cultural sensitivity, Respectful participation, Language and communication, Supporting local culture

2. Respect for Human Rights: Avoid exploitation, Respect dignity of all people, Support ethical businesses, Awareness of social issues

3. Environmental Responsibility: Reduce waste, Conserve resources, Respect natural environments, Support sustainable businesses, Minimize carbon footprint

4. Promoting Responsible Tourism: Educate fellow tourists, Choose sustainable souvenirs, Volunteer and give back, Respect local laws

vocabulary.definition: A person who moves from one place to another for work, leisure, or other purposes
A budget-conscious traveler who carries a backpack and explores destinations independently
A tourist who seeks thrilling and challenging experiences
Travel that respects places visited and people who live there
Understanding different customs and traditions to serve tourists better
Tourism that minimizes negative impacts and supports conservation

questions.description: During Activity: Group Participation and Contribution - Observe engagement and contribution. Peer Feedback after presentations. Observation Checklist on understanding of key concepts.

Written Reflection or Report summarizing learnings: Key behaviors expected in different cultural settings, Importance of respecting local cultures, Risks and concerns with reduction strategies, Significance of Tourist's Codes of behavior, Considerations for special needs tourists
Class Presentation/Discussion - Evaluate ability to present and explain findings, articulate cultural norms, explain behavior codes, provide solutions for travel risks
Essay questions on: Significance of respecting local cultures and traditions, Risks and concerns while traveling and how to reduce them
Written exercise from all activities covered
Quiz on the Tourist's Codes of behaviour
